archetype_reasoning: This trader is heavily concentrated in Russia-Ukraine and Iran-ME conflict themes, with 247 resolved trades across only 48 distinct markets. Directionality of 0.961 indicates near-uniform one-directional betting (predominantly NO on conflict escalation/ceasefires). The recent resolved trades show a cascade of losses on the same ceasefire-NO thesis across rolling date markets, a classic volume-specialist pattern of repeating the same thematic bet.
domain_blindspots: Europe: 31.2% accuracy — well below baseline, strong fade signal
Trump_US: 46.7% accuracy — marginally below coin-flip, no demonstrated edge
Iran_ME: 57.9% is modest but many recent losses on ceasefire-NO cascade suggest deteriorating edge
domain_summary: Genuine historical edge in Russia-Ukraine at 60.7% accuracy across 275 trades, likely reflecting deep situational knowledge of the conflict trajectory. China_Asia and LatAm show strong numbers but thin sample sizes; Europe and Trump_US should be discounted or faded.
signal_weight_reasoning: Strong Russia-UKR track record over large sample justifies domain-level signal weight. However, the extreme directionality (0.961 NO bias) and recent streak of ceasefire-NO losses — including some near-certainty entries at 0.992 and 0.999 — indicate possible anchoring and sunk-cost doubling-down. Signals outside Russia-UKR and possibly Iran-ME should be discounted given poor cross-domain accuracy.
behavioural_flags: Extreme directional bias: 96.1% of trades in one direction, almost exclusively NO on conflict resolution/ceasefires
Rolling date market chasing: repeatedly bets NO on same ceasefire question with advancing deadlines, suggesting conviction anchoring rather than fresh forecasting
Near-certainty entries: multiple trades at 0.999, 0.992, 0.93+ prices — minimal forecasting value at these levels
Position concentration: top open position is 17,683 shares on Iranian regime fall NO, representing massive single-thesis exposure
Doubling-down pattern: Israel-Hamas ceasefire NO traded at least 9 times across rolling markets, despite accumulating losses
risk_patterns: Sunk-cost anchoring: continued NO bets on ceasefire markets after consecutive losses, consistent with refusal to update priors
Yield harvesting crossover: several open positions at 0.91-0.99 NO prices blurring into near-certainty harvesting territory
Concentration risk: open book heavily weighted to Iran-ME with multiple correlated NO positions on US-Iran conflict escalation/resolution events
Recent resolved trades show 15 straight losses — may reflect a significant regime change in underlying conflict dynamics not yet incorporated into trading behavior
High ELO may partly reflect past Russia-UKR accuracy masking recent deterioration in Iran-ME domain

open_positions_summary: Open book is dominated by Iran-ME NO positions across multiple correlated markets (regime stability, US-Iran ceasefire, Kharg Island, uranium enrichment) totaling significant capital concentration. The unifying thesis is: no major Iran capitulation, no ceasefire, no regime change. Russia-Ukraine exposure is maintained via Peace Parlay NO and Pokrovsk territorial NO. A single YES position (US strikes Iran by Jan 14, 2026) suggests some hedging or conviction in limited escalation. The portfolio carries meaningful correlated drawdown risk if a US-Iran deal or major Iranian concession materialises.
watch_items: Monitor whether ceasefire-NO losing streak continues — 15 straight recent losses suggests thesis break, not bad luck
Iran diplomatic talks: any US-Iran deal would cascade losses across 5+ correlated open positions simultaneously
Russia x Ukraine Peace Parlay NO resolution by Dec 31 2026 — single largest Russia-UKR risk event
Whether trader adjusts directionality or continues doubling into NO positions despite losses
Iran regime stability NO at 0.636 — one of few genuinely contested price entries; monitor for position increases or exits as signal of conviction update
ELO decay trajectory: active ELO (2403) is already 263 points below peak ELO (2667), suggesting recency-weighted performance is declining
summary: This trader has a demonstrably strong Russia-Ukraine track record over a large sample (60.7% across 275 trades) and a LEGENDARY overall ELO, but the profile is significantly complicated by recent deterioration. A cascade of 15 consecutive losses — many in the core Iran-ME ceasefire-NO theme — combined with extreme directional bias (96.1%) and near-certainty yield harvesting entries suggests the trader is anchored to a geopolitical thesis that recent events have partially invalidated. Open positions carry heavy correlated Iran-ME risk. Signal value is HIGH for Russia-UKR directional calls at contested prices (0.10–0.80), but should be discounted or faded entirely in Europe, Trump_US, and near-certainty Iran positions.
